content: We can frame second-language vocabulary learning (the educational use of reflection in Topic A) as an iterative self-refinement process for human 'mental language models' that mirrors the LLM Self-Refine framework from Topic B. Just as LLMs improve outputs through structured self-feedback, language learners can use deliberate reflective self-checking to refine their implicit mental models of word usage, collocation, and context-dependent meaning.
